doc_name: Root Cause Analysis in the Age of Automated Workflows

chunks.text: When conducting process improvement, Root Cause Analysis is a physical, human-centric exercise. It involves stakeholders standing around a whiteboard, drawing Ishikawa diagrams, and asking the Five Whys until a human or mechanical point of failure is found. However, the nature of work has shifted as more industries are embracing automated workflows. Processes aren’t a string […]
